base_branch_or_worktree: codex/seo-agent-control-packet-01 in /private/tmp/fap-web-seo-agent-control-packet-01; control packet files are available but unmerged and uncommitted intent-to-add docs

status_definitions.READY_NOW: Executable immediately from the current authorized base with manifest/state authorization.
status_definitions.READY_AFTER_CONTROL_PACKET: Executable after SEO-AGENT-CONTROL-PACKET-01 is merged to main or the user explicitly authorizes using the control-packet branch as the base.
status_definitions.READY_AFTER_OPS_BRIDGE: Executable only after the ops read-model bridge has landed.
status_definitions.READY_AFTER_RUNTIME_QA: Executable only after public runtime SEO QA evidence exists.
status_definitions.READY_AFTER_GSC_QUALITY: Executable only after GSC data quality gates pass.
status_definitions.HOLD_CMS_SAFETY: Held because it could mutate CMS or editorial state without enough contracts and approvals.
status_definitions.HOLD_SEARCH_PROVIDER_SAFETY: Held because it could enqueue, approve, or submit to search providers without enough gates and approvals.
status_definitions.HOLD_NEEDS_MORE_EVIDENCE: Held because required evidence sources are missing, stale, mock, fixture, or unclassified.

candidate_prs.title: Bridge SEO operations dashboard to a sanitized read model
candidate_prs.objective: Replace hardcoded mock/static artifact assumptions in the fap-web SEO operations dashboard with a typed read-model boundary and explicit source labels, while preserving safe artifact fallback behavior.
candidate_prs.rationale: The dashboard currently displays mock/static artifact data. The agent must not make decisions from that surface until it can distinguish live read-model data from sample artifacts.
blockers: control packet not merged or not explicitly authorized as base
backend read-model endpoint/schema unknown
client-side credentials or private data required
forbidden_files: docs/seo/generated/** unless generated by the scoped check and declared
implementation_steps: Add a typed read-model adapter with source labels such as live_read_model, artifact_sample, mock_fixture, and unavailable.
Allow the route shell to pass a sanitized operations data prop into the dashboard.
Preserve empty/unknown values rather than converting missing metrics to zero.
Keep dashboard actions UI-only unless a later PR creates a reviewed backend action endpoint.
Add contract tests that forbid CMS writes, Search Channel submission, revalidation, env reads, cookies, private URLs, and raw PII.
stop_conditions: implementation needs CMS write, provider call, revalidation, or runtime sitemap/llms behavior changes
live endpoint returns raw PII/private/order/payment/attempt URLs
changed files drift outside declared scope
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Revert the adapter/dashboard prop wiring and restore explicit artifact_sample fallback; no CMS/search/provider state should exist to undo.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: If backend schema changes are needed, split into a fap-api read-model contract PR and a fap-web dashboard consumption PR.
human_authorization_required: authorize base if control packet is not merged

candidate_prs.title: Add public runtime SEO QA evidence generator
candidate_prs.objective: Create a read-only CLI that samples public URLs and emits bounded runtime SEO evidence for the control packet.
candidate_prs.rationale: URL inventory and generated artifacts do not prove public rendered canonical, robots, redirect, hreflang, JSON-LD, or noindex behavior. Runtime QA fills that evidence gap without mutating the site.
blockers: private/order/result/share/pay/take paths require live fetch
runtime anomaly would be used to auto-fix production behavior
control packet not merged or authorized
forbidden_files: Search Channel or provider submission paths
implementation_steps: Reuse existing live URL check helpers where possible.
Add pinned public URL samples and deny-policy samples for private paths.
Emit JSON with source classification, sampled URL, expected/actual status, canonical, noindex, robots, hreflang, JSON-LD, and anomaly severity.
Emit optional Markdown report for human review.
Ensure anomalies are evidence only and do not trigger fixes.
stop_conditions: live production QA is made CI-required without explicit approval
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Remove the CLI, samples, generated evidence artifact, and contract test; no runtime state should exist to undo.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Keep dashboard ingestion and anomaly remediation in separate later PRs.
human_authorization_required: authorize any live production sample run beyond read-only GETs

candidate_prs.title: Add GSC daily data quality contract
candidate_prs.objective: Add read-only quality checks for `seo_gsc_daily` before GSC-derived opportunity scoring is allowed.
candidate_prs.rationale: Opportunity detection cannot trust GSC data until row freshness, source state, query masking, duplicate keys, bounds, and fixture/live classification are explicit.
blockers: live GSC credentials or external API calls are required
collector writes are needed
raw queries or PII would be exposed
implementation_steps: Add a read-only report for row count by date/source_engine and latest report date.
Check data lag, null URL/query hash rates, duplicate keys, clicks/impressions/CTR/position bounds, and brand/non-brand split.
Scan output for forbidden raw query/PII fields.
Classify data state as live, fixture, stale, empty, or unknown.
Block downstream opportunity queue use unless the report passes.
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Remove the data-quality service/command/tests; no external or database writes should exist to undo.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: If missing indexes or migrations are required, split schema work into a separate fap-api PR.
human_authorization_required: authorize any live GSC API collection separately

candidate_prs.title: Add weekly automation control packet contract
candidate_prs.objective: Make the weekly SEO automation output a checkable control packet without editing local automation TOML.
candidate_prs.rationale: The weekly automation is already read-only. The next safe step is to define its packet output contract and checker before changing its schedule or prompt.
blockers: automation TOML edit requested without separate authorization
skill/context not available on merged main
packet schema lacks required automation context
implementation_steps: Add automation_context to the control packet schema if needed.
Define required weekly output blocks: human summary, CONTROL_PACKET_JSON, GPT55_HANDOFF_PROMPT, and approval matrix.
Add checker for read-only mode, source classifications, observation windows, hard holds, and GPT handoff presence.
Add an example packet update.
Document the separate local automation update approval path.
stop_conditions: checker permits mutation lanes without exact authorization
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Remove the docs/schema/checker/test changes; local automation config remains unchanged.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Keep local automation config update as a separate post-merge, explicitly authorized operation.
human_authorization_required: separate authorization for any local automation update

candidate_prs.title: Add GPT 5.5 Pro review handoff contract
candidate_prs.objective: Make GPT 5.5 Pro review output schema-checkable and non-executing.
candidate_prs.rationale: The user wants GPT 5.5 Pro to judge Codex plans. That handoff must be constrained to packet evidence and approval boundaries, not free-form execution advice.
blockers: GPT response schema lacks needed cross-reference checks
review prompt allows browsing or inferred analytics
review output can approve mutation lanes
implementation_steps: Add a paste-ready GPT 5.5 review prompt tied to the control packet.
Add an example GPT response JSON.
Add checker logic for schema shape, evidence ids, allowed lanes, claim-risk blocking, and approval boundaries.
Ensure GPT 5.5 Pro cannot approve CMS/search/schema/hreflang/indexability execution.
Document how Codex should consume GPT 5.5 output.
stop_conditions: review output is allowed to execute actions directly
checker accepts uncited evidence or inferred analytics
mutation lanes can be approved without exact human approval
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Remove the prompt/example/checker/test changes; no runtime state should exist to undo.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Keep prompt/schema/checker in one PR; runtime automation consumption remains later.

candidate_prs.title: Define read-only SEO opportunity queue contract
candidate_prs.objective: Define the opportunity queue data model, allowed evidence inputs, scoring constraints, and safety holds before implementing queue reads.
candidate_prs.rationale: An opportunity queue can easily become an unsafe action recommender if mock/GSC/CMS/search-provider evidence is not classified first.
blockers: GSC quality not yet proven for execution
CMS write path implied
Search Channel enqueue implied
implementation_steps: Define allowed evidence sources and source-classification requirements.
Define score inputs and what must remain unknown when data is missing.
Define no-action outputs: opportunity, rationale, evidence refs, confidence, and blocked actions.
Define downstream gates for readonly implementation, CMS draft package, and Search Channel readiness.
Add docs/tests only; no queue execution.
checks: JSON/YAML parse for any added schemas or manifest entries
pnpm or php contract checks only if repository-specific tests are added
stop_conditions: contract implies CMS writes or provider submissions
contract treats GSC as content/sitemap authority
contract permits mock/fixture data to drive actions
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Remove the contract docs/tests; no runtime state should exist to undo.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Keep contract separate from read-only implementation and dashboard display.

candidate_prs.title: Add read-only SEO opportunity queue
candidate_prs.objective: Implement a sanitized read-only opportunity queue from approved evidence sources after GSC quality gates pass.
candidate_prs.rationale: Opportunity scoring is useful only after data quality and contracts prove the queue is not driven by stale, fixture, mock, or private evidence.
blockers: GSC data quality incomplete
opportunity contract incomplete
ops dashboard still relies on mock/static artifact data
likely_files: backend/routes/api.php only if a read-only endpoint is explicitly authorized
forbidden_files: fap-web dashboard display in the same PR
implementation_steps: Read approved evidence sources only.
Emit sanitized queue items with evidence ids, confidence, source state, blocked actions, and next safe lane.
Exclude raw queries, private URLs, private ids, payment/order data, and provider credentials.
Keep output read-only with no action endpoint.
Add tests for empty/stale/fixture/missing-data states.
stop_conditions: GSC quality report is failing, stale, fixture-only, or unknown
implementation mutates CMS or Search Channel state
raw private evidence appears in output
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Remove the read-only service/endpoint/tests; no CMS/search/provider state should exist to undo.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Implement backend read-only queue first; fap-web dashboard display is a later PR.

candidate_prs.title: Define SEO CMS draft package contract
candidate_prs.objective: Define the dry-run package contract, claim gates, approval matrix, and rollback path for future CMS draft package pilots.
candidate_prs.rationale: CMS draft work must not start from free-form agent text. It needs a package contract owned by GPT 5.5 Pro for content judgment and validated by Codex for import safety.
blockers: content body generation requested from Codex
draft write implied before dry-run contract
publish/index/search action implied
forbidden_files: article body content generation by Codex
implementation_steps: Define required package fields, locale expectations, translation group rules, active-surface guard, and claim gate.
Define draft-only/noindex/no-sitemap/no-llms/schema-hold/hreflang-hold/search-hold flags.
Define exact approval phrases for dry-run, draft write, publish, and search release.
Define rollback path and evidence output.
Add docs/tests only; no CMS writes.
checks: JSON/YAML parse for schemas and manifest entries
php artisan test --filter=ArticleImportSeoContentPackageDraftCommandTest if backend contract tests are added
stop_conditions: implementation generates publishable article body content
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Remove the contract docs/tests; no CMS state should exist to undo.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Keep package contract separate from any pilot import.

candidate_prs.title: Pilot SEO CMS draft package dry run
candidate_prs.objective: Run a one-package, draft-only CMS package pilot after contract, content approval, and exact dry-run/write approvals exist.
candidate_prs.rationale: This touches CMS state and therefore cannot be bundled with contracts, read-model work, or opportunity scoring.
dependencies: exact package path and hash approval
blockers: package contract missing
content approval missing
exact package hash missing
dry-run approval missing
implementation_steps: Select one approved package path and hash.
Run package validation and dry-run import only.
If and only if separately approved, write draft state without publish/index/search release.
Capture article id, diff, rollback path, and hold flags.
Stop before publish or search release.
stop_conditions: Codex is asked to write article body content
package hash or approval phrase missing
draft write is requested before dry-run approval
publish/index/search/schema/hreflang is requested in same PR
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Use CMS draft delete/revert path documented by the package contract; publish/search state must remain untouched.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Keep dry-run, draft write, publish, and search release as separate approvals and usually separate PRs.
human_authorization_required: separate publish approval if ever needed

candidate_prs.title: Add Search Channel readiness gates
candidate_prs.objective: Define and test Search Channel readiness/preflight gates without enqueueing, approving, or submitting URLs.
candidate_prs.rationale: Search provider submissions have external side effects and should never be automatic from an SEO opportunity alone.
blockers: runtime QA missing
GSC quality missing
URL truth/indexability not proven
exact URL/channel approval missing
implementation_steps: Add readiness report for candidate_count, eligible_count, planned_queue_count, reason_code breakdown, duplicate detection, and gate states.
Add dry-run preflight only.
Validate no external_calls and no search_submission.
Document exact approval phrases for enqueue, approval, and live submission as separate gates.
Stop before live provider calls.
stop_conditions: implementation writes queue entries without exact approval
candidate URL is private, noindex, claim-unsafe, or not backend-authoritative
candidate_prs.rollback_path: Readiness/preflight PR should create no external state; if a queue row is ever created in a later PR, use Search Channel retry/reset/cancel path documented by that PR.
candidate_prs.split_recommendation: Split readiness, queue write, approval, and live submission into separate gates.

parallelizable.constraints: Use separate worktrees and one PR per scope.
Do not edit automation TOML or build opportunity queue execution.

hold_items.reason: Wait for GSC data quality, opportunity contract, and ops read-model bridge.

unhold_when: GPT 5.5 Pro approves exact package content
user approves exact package path and hash

required_authorizations: Authorize each PR id before adding it to docs/codex/pr-train.yaml.
Authorize each state entry before adding it to docs/codex/pr-train-state.json.
Confirm whether execution should wait for merged main or use the unmerged control-packet branch as base.
Authorize local automation TOML edits separately by automation id, file path, merged SHA, and prompt-contract version.
Authorize CMS dry-run, CMS draft write, publish, and search release as separate steps.
Authorize Search Channel readiness, queue write, approval, and live submission as separate steps.
Authorize any live external API calls or provider credentials separately.
uncertain_items: Exact fap-api read-model schema consumed by fap-web must be confirmed before live dashboard consumption.
GSC table may be empty, stale, fixture-only, or incomplete until SEO-GSC-DATA-QUALITY-01 proves otherwise.
Weekly automation should not be changed until control packet support is merged and available from repo truth.
Search Channel Queue execution services exist, but queue writes and provider calls remain out of scope for this decomposition.
CMS draft pilot needs exact package content approval and package hash; Codex should not generate the publishable article body.
